title: [緊急] 612 組織BC及BNO人到英領遞交聯署信

replies.msg:
六月九日 百萬人遊行 英國已率先出招 今晚國會將就香港情況進行緊急討論
承接較前個Post: https://lihkg.com/thread/1199369/page/7


本人及一眾BC/BNO會員打算緊急組織六月十二日到香港英國領使館遞交聯署信比使館代表 如可以想各位帶同自己既護照出席 到時最後同代表合照之用 (如真的成事)

現急需一眾BC及BNO會員提供以下恊助：
i) 英文叻人草擬一封聯署信，預留足夠空位或空頁，到時即場給眾人自己簽名及寫上自己護照號碼，最後放入信封，封口，直接交比使館代表。無任何影印本或內容的照片留低，確保參與者個人資料不會流出。
ii) 需要英文人或有人脈人士幫手事先知會英國領使館，同睇下咩時間方便佢地出黎收信。
iii) 需要人幫手聯絡或知會 Hong Kong Watch 有關是次請願 歡迎他們派代表出席 然後在他們的FB發佈 當然亦希望佢地幫手搵到西方傳媒採訪
iiii) 需要有BC及BNO既你係度參與, 睇下有幾多人..太少就...算了吧

重點:是次係和平請願 並無留守或佔領打算 重點是表明在港英國國民之立場,表忠及中英聯合聲明已死 在港英人有實在被迫害既風險

其餘歡迎繼續討論/商討

quote.msg: 封信大概咩內容？英文系學生想幫手

replies.msg:
暫時打到咁...
個人想法 主要FOCUS ON 今次修例係表示中共違反中英聯合聲明, 英國國民在港安全受威脅

June 12, 2019
Andrew Heyn OBE
Consul General to Hong Kong & Macao,
British Consulate-General Hong Kong

Petition Letter
by a Group of British Citizens and British Nationals (Overseas) in Hong Kong

Your Excellency,

We are writing urgently to express our deepest concern for the extradition law being recently put forward by the government of Hong Kong.

It is obvious that this extradition law would compromise Hong Kong’s high degree of autonomy and its independence of jurisdiction on which Hong Kong’s success depends as it would allow communist China to bypass Hong Kong’s legal system to detain whoever in Hong Kong Beijing find “necessary”, including thousands of British Citizens and British Nationals in the region.

This is also a fundamental breach of the Sino-British Joint Declaration and the One Country Two Systems enshrined in the Basic Laws. 仲打緊

replies.msg:
~formal各國家公開信上款

~第一段(簡而精2句用香港人的情感動人)
"尋日一百萬人抵住濕熱天氣上街，2點至半夜，而港府只係11點冷冷地出左個聲明重申不變"

~第2段(搵人幫手多謝人先)
"多謝各國近來關心此事件,eg.各領事發聲明/外交照會"

~第3段(問題所在)
香港人的民主制度決失令到港府可以恣意不理一百萬人請求同各國意見，香港人已盡力而為，一百萬人香港人別無他法，要尋求國際協助

~第4段(利害關係)
"此事都影響各國在港國民性命安全財產。。。"

~第5段(具體協助方式)
第一階段: 如果修例通過，制裁所有投票立法會議員/香港政府高層和其家屬，包括
***加入入境黑名單/不受歡迎人物(唔批簽證比佢地去旅行，以後剩係遊香港/大陸lolll)
***凍結其資產
***甚至x奪其國民身份，強制出境
第二階段:中共高層
其他:取消港美關係法/引渡協議。。。

結尾: 再多謝下感動下

Appendix: 全體建制派立法會議員個名

聯署信draft - 求討論/指教
**************************************************
June 12, 2019

Andrew Heyn OBE
Consul General to Hong Kong & Macao,
British Consulate-General Hong Kong


Petition Letter
by a Group of British Citizens and British Nationals (Overseas) in Hong Kong

Your Excellency,

We are writing urgently to express our deepest concern for a currently proposed change to the Fugitives Offenders Ordinance (often referred to as Extradition Law) put forward by the government of Hong Kong.

On 9th Jun 2019, a million of Hong Kong people has marched a day and night onto the streets to protest against the introduction of this proposed extradition law. Hong Kong currently has extradition agreements with 18 countries, including the UK and four other EU member states. The proposed amendment will extend extradition coverage to laws of communist mainland China. This bill, if passed, will pose an imminent threat to civil rights of and the very safety to British nationals with connections to or living in Hong Kong.

Although the Chief Executive Carrie Lam has given assurances that there will be room for further discussions and there will be safeguards in place, we do not trust the legal system in China nor do we trust that the government of Hong Kong will stand up against Chinese authorities.

We ask, if this bill passed the Legislative Council, the UK government consider:
• to take further action to oppose the introduction of this extradition law for Hong Kong’s high degree of autonomy and freedoms enshrined in the Sino-British Joint Declaration;
• to examine the possibility for BN(O) passport holders to reside in the UK and/or the Commonwealth member states.

Yours Sincerely,
A Group of British Citizens and British Nationals (Overseas) in Hong Kong

後加各位BC及BNO既簽名和PASSPORT NO.

根據樓主篇野打左少少，仲差一part係想佢地出手幫，但應該叫佢地點幫好？

Re: Concern of the Own Safety of British Citizens and British Nationals (Overseas) in Hong Kong

Dear Honorable Ambassador,

I am writing concerning our own safety regarding the recent plan to allow extradition to Mainland Chine proposed by HKSAR government.

The controversial extradition law amendment plans will seriously damage the high degree of autonomy of Hong Kong and most importantly, the judicial independence constitutionally entrenched in Basic Law, as it is believed that when the extradition law is enforced, it can be a strong weapon by the Beijing government to eliminate the voices against their regime by allowing suspected criminals to be sent to mainland China to face trail, and it therefore inevitably threatens the freedom and even own safety of Hong Kong citizens, including the British citizens and British Nationals living in the city.

We would like to express our fierce opposition to this proposal. Hundreds of thousands of people who took part in the march against the law on 6/9/2019, however, do not get a proper response from the government as the amendment will not be delayed or scraped despite of the biggest demonstration since the 1997 handover. We believe Hong Kong is our home and have no intention to leave. Obviously the amendment has no way to close the “legal loophole” as the government has claimed, but violate the Sino-British Joint Declaration and diminish Hong Kong’s jurisdiction under “one country, two systems”. Most importantly, it poses a huge threat to the own safety of Hong Kong residents, where it is considered that the judicial systems in mainland China can be manipulated for political reasons and residents sent will not receive fair trial.

***********************************
June 12, 2019

Andrew Heyn OBE
Consul General to Hong Kong & Macao,
British Consulate-General Hong Kong


Petition Letter
by a Group of British Citizens and British Nationals (Overseas) in Hong Kong

Your Excellency,

We are writing urgently to express our deepest concern for a currently proposed change to the Fugitives Offenders Ordinance (often referred to as Extradition Law) put forward by the government of Hong Kong.

On 9th Jun 2019, a million of Hong Kong people has marched a day and night onto the streets to protest against the introduction of this proposed extradition law. Hong Kong currently has extradition agreements with 18 countries, including the UK and four other EU member states. The proposed amendment will extend extradition coverage to laws of communist mainland China. This bill, if passed, will pose an imminent threat to civil rights of and the very safety to British nationals with connections to or living in Hong Kong.

Although the Chief Executive Carrie Lam has given assurances that there will be room for further discussions and there will be safeguards in place, we do not trust the legal system in China nor do we trust that the government of Hong Kong will stand up against Chinese authorities.

We ask, if this bill was passed by the Legislative Council, the UK government consider:
• to take further action to oppose the introduction of this extradition law for Hong Kong’s high degree of autonomy and freedoms enshrined in the Sino-British Joint Declaration;
• to examine the possibility for BN(O) passport holders to reside in the UK and/or the Commonwealth member states.

Yours Sincerely,
A Group of British Citizens and British Nationals (Overseas) in Hong Kong

We ask, if this bill was passed by the Legislative Council, the UK government consider:

• to take further actions to oppose the introduction of this extradition law for Hong Kong’s high degree of autonomy and freedoms enshrined in the Sino-British Joint Declaration;

• to officially declare a breach by China on the Sino-British Joint Declaration should the government of Hong Kong and Beijing continue to neglect the further actions taken by the UK and the international communities; also

• to consider, in the United Nations, the possibility of voiding the United Nations General Assembly Resolution 2908 that deprived Hong Kong people of the right to self-determination;

• to examine the possibility for BN(O) passport holders to reside in the UK and/or some member states of the Commonwealth. Some British overseas territories such as Gibraltar and the Falkland Islands might also be considered.

***********************************
June 12, 2019

Andrew Heyn OBE
Consul General to Hong Kong & Macao,
British Consulate-General Hong Kong


Petition Letter
by a Group of British Citizens and British Nationals (Overseas) in Hong Kong

Your Excellency,

We are writing urgently to express our deepest concern for a currently proposed change to the Fugitives Offenders Ordinance (often referred to as Extradition Law) put forward by the government of Hong Kong.

On 9th Jun 2019, a million of Hong Kong people has marched a day and night onto the streets to protest against the introduction of this proposed extradition law. Hong Kong currently has extradition agreements with 18 countries, including the UK and four other EU member states. The proposed amendment will extend extradition coverage to laws of communist mainland China. This bill, if passed, will pose an imminent threat to civil rights of and the very safety to British nationals with connections to or living in Hong Kong.

Although the Chief Executive Carrie Lam has given assurances that there will be room for further discussions and there will be safeguards in place, we do not trust the legal system in China nor do we trust that the government of Hong Kong will stand up against Chinese authorities.

We ask, if this bill was passed by the Legislative Council, the UK government consider:

• to take further actions to oppose the introduction of this extradition law for Hong Kong’s high degree of autonomy and freedoms enshrined in the Sino-British Joint Declaration;

• to officially declare a breach by China on the Sino-British Joint Declaration should the government of Hong Kong and Beijing continue to neglect the further actions taken by the UK and the international communities; also

• to consider, in the United Nations, the possibility of voiding the United Nations General Assembly Resolution 2908 that deprived Hong Kong people of the right to self-determination;

• to examine to expand the right of BN(O) passport holders to reside in the UK and/or some member states of the Commonwealth. Some British overseas territories such as Gibraltar and the Falkland Islands might also be considered.

Yours Sincerely,
A Group of British Citizens and British Nationals (Overseas) in Hong Kong

[求識外語者或熟外國文化既人幫拖]想用"由聽日開始外國人黎香港有機會俾人捉番大陸"黎做標題po去唔同國家既論壇同social media

其實今次過左既話外國遊客或者在港外藉人士有可能受影響，同外國人傾個陣發覺其實好難單從香港人人權受影響去令人留意，所以想睇下有冇機會用依樣野黎發大件事黎攪，想要用依樣黎做標題引多d外國既市民注意，再提供當地既請願方式去攪大件事從而令各地政府去開始承受較大壓力，去開始有更具體既操作，我見對外既大家都係支持香港之類既post所以想睇下有冇新方向可以再攪大佢
主要係想不斷槓桿式攪大件事，因為如果開始多外國市民知先有機會令外國政府更大行動，但依家大部分外國人其實都唔知，所以有機既話哪怕係令外國人去幫手簽d人權聯署去增加影響力都好去令各地政府有更大壓力都好，當然講個陣要套用返各個地方既例子去做類比就最好
意思係想如果係會既話，咁不如用"由聽日開始外國人黎香港有機會俾人捉番大陸"黎做標題po去唔同國家論壇同social media，咁起碼會好多好多有相關野既，例如家人係香港或者會黎工作既會留意，再補條要求當地政府關注既link去攪大件事去，又舉個例，日本後生一代好中意黎香港玩，你見唔少日本明星都有，最近日本節目先講完，如果係2ch出到依類post既話我相信係日本迴響會比依家大好多，其他地方都係
有冇人可以幫手

Template類似
由聽日開始外國人黎香港旅行有機會俾人捉番大陸
香港最為人熟悉既李小龍、成龍，一個充滿東方味道及美麗夜景同中國完全不同既地方。係過往香港同大陸係唔同體制，但香港政府係聽日有機會通過一條法例，從而令任何人係香港即使旅客、工作中既外國人都有機會俾人捉番大陸，係6月9日有一百萬人上街遊行但香港政府仍然堅持通過，而香港依家有如(XX某地方類似性質既歷史事件)一樣，依個條例會剝奪香港人既包括生命權、自由權、財產權、尊嚴權及追求幸福的各項人權，而且不受任何人權法律保障。由於香港政府聽命於內地政府，所以需要各國政府動用國際關係去保護21世紀仲會大倒退既人權，特別香港同中國一直行緊兩套政策同完全唔同既文化語言。所以懇請各位曾經對香港文化有認識或者黎過香港，又或尊重人權認為人人平等既各位，可以幫手請願各個政府有關注事件，利用國際關係同具體措施去對香港同中國政府進行施壓，特別月尾前會通過所以情況緊急，可以的話請幫忙廣傳。
(之後提供當地簡單既官方請願方式)

居港BC及BNO港人聯署活動Update:

簽名時間: 09:00 至 約17:00, 6月12日
地點: 金鐘正義道英領使館門外門口
參加者: 請帶同英國護照(作身份析別，防五毛白撞，義工不會要求你展示內頁) 到門口找人員簽名，聯署信會在約下午五時封口入信封直接交予領使館代表。
目的：就港府提出的修訂逃犯條例：
要求英國重新審視聯合國2908決議案還香港自決權
要求英國擴大BN(O)子女繼承權
要求英國, 萬一修訂通過, 審視覓地收容在港BN(O)國民

叫你地嘅外國朋友，尤其係亞洲國家，唔理菲律賓印尼印度以色列土耳其，總之係喺香港住嘅外國朋友。幫手寫信俾佢地所屬領事館。
Template 如下：

Attn: HE(Name of Counsul-General)
Consul General of (your country name) in Hong Kong

Your Excellency,

I am a (your country name) citizen currently residing in Hong Kong, and am writing to you to express my deep concern regarding the proposed amendment of Extradition Law in Hong Kong. The amendment would allow Hong Kong Government to extradite fugitives to countries and regions that are not signatory of formal extradition treaty with Hong Kong, including China mainland.



According to pro-Beijing newspaper Wenwei Po, citing source close to the central government, Chinese authorities could demand Hong Kong government to extradite foreign nationals who were considered a threat to national security to mainland China. However, it is clear from past experience that Chinese authorities had abused National Security Law to restrict freedom of speech, also to censor and persecute dissidents. The Extradition Law would effectively extend applicability of Chinese National Security Law to Hong Kong.

This raised the possibility that (your country name) citizens residing in Hong Kong would be exploited by Chinese government as political hostages and extradited to China on dubious and even fabricated charges. Since it is highly unlikely that any law courts in mainland China could provide a fair trial to defendants, due to heavy political influence on Chinese judicial system, the Extradition Law is a true threat to personal safety, assets and freedom of (country name) citizens currently living in Hong Kong.



On the basis of such, I wish you could reflect my concerns to the Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Your Excellency's country and the corresponding Central Government. I also wish Your Excellency's Government could also join the rank of American and European allies to take an official stance against the amendment of Extradition Law.

Thank you very much for spending time to read this and your attention.



Regards,


Mr. X (passport number: X)

Mr. Y (passport number: Y)

Ms. Z (passport number: Z)



12th June, 2019


地址喺到：
https://www.protocol.gov.hk/eng/consular/asia.htm
